revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこく...'
revision.text:
'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう)は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳|仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳|孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 ==
=== 祖先 === 『[[日本書紀]]』によると、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦|豊城入彦命]]が東国統治を命じられ、[[上毛野国造]]や[[下毛野国造]]などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行|景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子[[御諸別王]]も引き続き、善政を行ったという。『[[旧事本紀]]』によると、[[仁徳|仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の４世孫の[[奈良別]]が下毛国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下野氏|下毛野氏]]。[[姓]]は[[君]]。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に[[下毛野]]姓が与えられた。
=== 本拠 === [[下野国]]。現在の[[栃木県]]。
=== 氏神 === [[二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[日光市]]。祭神は[[二荒山神]]。下野国一宮。 [[二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[宇都宮市]]。祭神は[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。
[[赤城神社]] …… 栃木県[[佐野市]]。祭神は彦狭嶋王。
=== 子孫 === *[[下毛野子麻呂]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] …… [[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。
*[[下毛野年継]] …… [[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公忠]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下野国|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:古墳時代|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]]

'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう)は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳|仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳|孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == [[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦|豊城入彦命]]を祖とし[[出雲神]]を祀る一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''くに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
=== 祖先 === 『[[日本書紀]]』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行|景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子[[御諸別王]]も引き続き、善政を行ったという。『[[旧事本紀]]』によると、[[仁徳|仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の４世孫の[[奈良別]]が下毛国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下野氏|下毛野氏]]。[[姓]]は[[君]]。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に[[下毛野]]姓が与えられた。
=== 本拠 === [[下野国]]。現在の[[栃木県]]。
=== 氏神 === *[[日光二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[日光市]]。祭神は[[二荒山神]]。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[宇都宮市]]。祭神は[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] …… 栃木県[[佐野市]]。祭神は彦狭嶋王。
=== 子孫 === *[[下毛野古麻呂]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] …… [[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。
*[[下毛野年継]] …… [[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公忠]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。

{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]]

'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう)は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳|仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳|孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == [[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦|豊城入彦命]]を祖とし[[出雲神]]を祀る一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''くに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
=== 祖先 === 『[[日本書紀]]』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行|景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子[[御諸別王]]も引き続き、善政を行ったという。『[[旧事本紀]]』によると、[[仁徳|仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の４世孫の[[奈良別]]が下毛国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下野氏|下毛野氏]]。[[姓]]は[[君]]。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に[[下毛野]]姓が与えられた。
=== 本拠 === [[下野国]]。現在の[[栃木県]]。
=== 氏神 === *[[日光二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[日光市]]。祭神は[[二荒山神]]。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[宇都宮市]]。祭神は[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] …… 栃木県[[佐野市]]。祭神は彦狭嶋王。
=== 子孫 === *[[下毛野古麻呂]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] …… [[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。
*[[下毛野年継]] …… [[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公忠]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう)は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == [[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]を祖とし[[出雲神]]を祀る一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''くに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
=== 祖先 === 『[[日本書紀]]』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子[[御諸別王]]も引き続き、善政を行ったという。『[[旧事本紀]]』によると、仁徳天皇朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の[[奈良別]]が下毛国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下野氏|下毛野氏]]。[[姓]]は[[君]]。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に[[下毛野]]姓が与えられた。
=== 本拠 === [[下野国]]。現在の[[栃木県]]。
=== 氏神 === *[[日光二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[日光市]]。祭神は[[二荒山神]]。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[宇都宮市]]。祭神は[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] …… 栃木県[[佐野市]]。祭神は彦狭嶋王。
=== 子孫 === *[[下毛野古麻呂]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] …… [[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。
*[[下毛野年継]] …… [[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:国造|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下毛野氏|*しもつけの]]

contributor.username: ja>壁掛成蛸は三考する葦である

revision.comment: 下毛野氏のリンクを修正。
revision.text:
'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう)は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == [[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]を祖とし[[出雲神]]を祀る一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''くに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
=== 祖先 === 『[[日本書紀]]』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子[[御諸別王]]も引き続き、善政を行ったという。『[[旧事本紀]]』によると、仁徳天皇朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の[[奈良別]]が下毛国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下毛野氏]]。[[姓]]は[[君]]。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に下毛野姓が与えられた。
=== 本拠 === [[下野国]]。現在の[[栃木県]]。
=== 氏神 === *[[日光二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[日光市]]。祭神は[[二荒山神]]。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] …… 栃木県[[宇都宮市]]。祭神は[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] …… 栃木県[[佐野市]]。祭神は彦狭嶋王。
=== 子孫 === *[[下毛野古麻呂]] …… [[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] …… [[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。
*[[下毛野年継]] …… [[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] …… [[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:国造|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下毛野氏|*しもつけの]]

'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう)は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』では'''下毛野君'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''下毛野国造'''と表記される。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』によると、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子・'''[[豊木入日子命]]'''が上毛野、下毛野君等の祖とされる。 *『日本書紀』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子'''[[御諸別王]]'''も引き続き、善政を行ったという。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によると、[[仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の'''[[奈良別]]'''が下毛国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下毛野氏]]（しもつけぬうじ、[[姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]）。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に下毛野姓が与えられた。
== 本拠 == 国造の本拠は[[宇都宮市]]周辺か。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''下毛野国'''と呼ばれていた地域、後の[[那須国]]を除いた[[下野国]]と同じ領域で、現在の[[栃木県]]の南西部に当たる。
[[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]を祖とし[[大国主神|出雲神]]を祀る一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''のくに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
== 氏神 == *[[日光二荒山神社]] *:[[栃木県]][[日光市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の二荒山神（[[大己貴命]]、[[田心姫命]]、[[味耜高彦根命]]）。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] *:栃木県[[宇都宮市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] *:栃木県[[佐野市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[彦狭嶋王]]。
== 子孫 == *[[下毛野古麻呂]] *:[[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] *:[[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。 *[[下毛野年継]] *:[[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:国造|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下毛野氏|*しもつけの]]

'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう）は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』では'''下毛野君'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''下毛野国造'''と表記される。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』によると、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子・'''[[豊木入日子命]]'''が上毛野、下毛野君等の祖とされる。 *『日本書紀』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子'''[[御諸別王]]'''も引き続き、善政を行ったという。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によると、[[仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の'''[[奈良別]]'''が下毛野国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下毛野氏]]（しもつけぬうじ、[[姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]）。[[天武天皇]]13年([[684年]])に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年([[783年]])には降伏した蝦夷に下毛野姓が与えられた。

=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''下毛野国'''と呼ばれていた地域、後の[[那須国]]を除いた[[下野国]]と同じ領域で、現在の[[栃木県]]の南西部に当たる。

== 子孫 == *[[下毛野古麻呂]] *:[[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] *:[[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。 *[[下毛野年継]] *:[[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう）は[[下野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』では'''下毛野君'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''下毛野国造'''と表記される。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』によると、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子・'''[[豊木入日子命]]'''が上毛野、下毛野君等の祖とされる。 *『日本書紀』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子'''[[御諸別王]]'''も引き続き、善政を行ったという。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によると、[[仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の'''[[奈良別]]'''が下毛野国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下毛野氏]]（しもつけぬうじ、[[姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]）。[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）には降伏した蝦夷に下毛野姓が与えられた。
== 本拠 == 国造の本拠は[[宇都宮市]]周辺か。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''下毛野国'''と呼ばれていた地域、後の[[那須国]]を除いた[[下野国]]と同じ領域で、現在の[[栃木県]]の南西部に当たる。
[[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]を祖とする一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''のくに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
== 氏神 == *[[日光二荒山神社]] *:[[栃木県]][[日光市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の二荒山神（[[大己貴命]]、[[田心姫命]]、[[味耜高彦根命]]）。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] *:栃木県[[宇都宮市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] *:栃木県[[佐野市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[彦狭嶋王]]。
== 子孫 == *[[下毛野古麻呂]] *:[[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] *:[[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。 *[[下毛野年継]] *:[[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:国造|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下毛野氏|*しもつけの]]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku. == Overview == === Naming === The title appears as '..."
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
[[Category:Japanese historical stubs]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Kuninomiyatsuko]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[ja:下毛野国造]]

revision.comment: adding original pages categories to all the pages I newly created today
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."

His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.

=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."

His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

[[Category:bump]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:下毛野国造]]

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

[[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:下毛野国造]]

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: attributing page creation to myself as per first edit summary
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

[[Category:bump]] [[Category:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[ja:下毛野国造]]

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
