revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.

The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.

A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."

His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.

Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|下毛野氏" to "|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan"
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."

His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Important original categories]] not categories using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|12=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|12=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|12=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

{{draft categories| [[Category:japanese clans]] }} [[sn:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:下毛野国造]] [[sn:himotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|12=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|12=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|12=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
