revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=draft|12=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft|12=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|12=draft|12=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|12=draft|12=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=draft|12=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. == Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

[[sn:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:下毛野国造]] [[sn:himotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|12=draft|12=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=draft|12=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|12=draft|12=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=draft|12=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft|12=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=draft|12=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=draft|12=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=draft|12=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|12=draft|12=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=draft|12=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|12=draft|12=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|12=draft|12=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|12=draft|12=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=draft|12=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=draft|12=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=draft|12=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=draft|12=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|12=draft|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=draft|12=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=draft|12=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

[[sn:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:下毛野国造]] [[sn:himotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
{{citation needed|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}
<!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> <!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:下毛野国造 up to rev 103922863
revision.text:
'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう）は[[下野国|下毛野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』では'''下毛野君'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''下毛野国造'''と表記される。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』によると、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子・'''[[豊木入日子命]]'''が上毛野、下毛野君等の祖とされる。 *『日本書紀』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子'''[[御諸別王]]'''も引き続き、善政を行ったという。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によると、[[仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の'''[[奈良別]]'''が下毛野国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下毛野氏]]（しもつけのうじ、[[姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]）。[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）には降伏した蝦夷に下毛野姓が与えられた。
== 本拠 == 国造の本拠は[[宇都宮市|小山市飯塚]]周辺か。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''下毛野国'''と呼ばれていた地域、後の[[那須国]]を除いた[[下野国]]と同じ領域で、現在の[[栃木県]]の南西部に当たる。
[[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]を祖とする一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''のくに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
== 氏神 == *[[日光二荒山神社]] *:[[栃木県]][[日光市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の二荒山神（[[大己貴命]]、[[田心姫命]]、[[味耜高彦根命]]）。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] *:栃木県[[宇都宮市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] *:栃木県[[佐野市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[彦狭嶋王]]。
== 子孫 == *[[下毛野古麻呂]] *:[[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] *:[[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。 *[[下毛野年継]] *:[[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:国造|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下毛野氏|*しもつけの]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[下毛野国造]] to [[Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''下毛野国造'''（しもみつけぬのくにのみやつこ・しもつけぬこくぞう・しもつけののくにのみやつこ・しもつけのこくぞう）は[[下野国|下毛野国]]を支配した[[国造]]。もと[[毛野国造]]で、[[仁徳天皇]]朝～[[孝徳天皇]]朝に[[上毛野国造]]と分裂した。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[古事記]]』では'''下毛野君'''、『[[日本書紀]]』、『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」では'''下毛野国造'''と表記される。
=== 祖先 === *『古事記』によると、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子・'''[[豊木入日子命]]'''が上毛野、下毛野君等の祖とされる。 *『日本書紀』によると、崇神天皇の皇子の豊城入彦命が東国統治を命じられ、上毛野国造や下毛野国造などの祖先になったという。また、その孫の[[彦狭嶋王]]が[[景行天皇]]朝に[[東山道]]十五国都督に任じられ、その子'''[[御諸別王]]'''も引き続き、善政を行ったという。 *『先代旧事本紀』「国造本紀」によると、[[仁徳天皇]]朝に豊城入彦命の4世孫の'''[[奈良別]]'''が下毛野国造に任じられたという。
=== 氏族 === [[下毛野氏]]（しもつけのうじ、[[姓]]は[[キミ (カバネ)|君]]）。[[天武天皇]]13年（[[684年]]）に[[朝臣]]を賜った。[[延暦]]2年（[[783年]]）には降伏した蝦夷に下毛野姓が与えられた。
== 本拠 == 国造の本拠は[[宇都宮市|小山市飯塚]]周辺か。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''下毛野国'''と呼ばれていた地域、後の[[那須国]]を除いた[[下野国]]と同じ領域で、現在の[[栃木県]]の南西部に当たる。
[[古代]]、[[香取海]]に注ぐ[[鬼怒川|毛野川]]流域には、[[崇神天皇]]の皇子[[豊城入彦命]]を祖とする一大豪族が[[毛野国]]（'''けの'''のくに）を形成し、[[大和朝廷]]においても強大な発言力を有していたが、その後毛野国は下毛野国と上毛野国（[[上野国]]）に分割された。古来より下毛野国は香取海に注ぐ毛野川流域であったが、[[利根川東遷事業]]により[[利根川]]に注ぐ毛野川流域地域となり、領域は現在の[[栃木県]]とほぼ同じである。
== 氏神 == *[[日光二荒山神社]] *:[[栃木県]][[日光市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の二荒山神（[[大己貴命]]、[[田心姫命]]、[[味耜高彦根命]]）。下野国一宮。 *[[宇都宮二荒山神社]] *:栃木県[[宇都宮市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[豊城入彦命]]ほか。下野国一宮。 *[[赤城神社]] *:栃木県[[佐野市]]に鎮座する神社。祭神は国造祖の[[彦狭嶋王]]。
== 子孫 == *[[下毛野古麻呂]] *:[[飛鳥時代]]の官人。[[忍壁皇子|刑部親王]]・[[藤原不比等]]・[[粟田真人]]らとともに[[大宝律令]]を選定。 *[[下毛野虫麻呂]] *:[[奈良時代]]の学者。式部員外少輔。文章に優れていた。 *[[下毛野年継]] *:[[平安時代]]の官人。官奴正。従五位上。 *[[下毛野公時]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。相撲使。[[藤原道長]]の[[随身]]。金太郎のモデルといわれる。 *[[下毛野公忠]] *:[[平安時代]]の武人。近衛舎人。[[藤原頼通]]の護衛。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） *『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） *『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{DEFAULTSORT:しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]] [[Category:下野国]] [[Category:栃木県の歴史]] [[Category:国造|しもつけぬのくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:下毛野氏|*しもつけの]]

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
{{citation needed|Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko}}

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ame-no-hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.

In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.

It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinugawa River|qq=}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Sea|qq=}}. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintarō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yorimichi|qq=}}.

<!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko-->

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=}}, an area that later became {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|ja|下野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsuke Province|qq=}}. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}}'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|qq=}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the {{ill|Tōsandō|ja|東山道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsandō|qq=}} under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sendai Kuji Hongi|qq=}} states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|qq=}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of {{ill|Asomi|ja|朝臣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asomi|qq=}}. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day {{ill|Utsunomiya|ja|宇都宮市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya|qq=}}.
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|qq=}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|ja|栃木県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tochigi Prefecture|qq=}}.
In antiquity, the basin of the {{ill|Kinugawa River|ja|鬼怒川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinugawa River|qq=}}, then called Kenogawa, flowed into {{ill|Katori Sea|ja|香取海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Sea|qq=}}. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * {{ill|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|ja|日光二荒山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Futarasan Shrine|qq=}} *: Located in {{ill|Nikkō|ja|日光市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * {{ill|Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|ja|宇都宮二荒山神社|1=Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Futarasan Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|qq=}} *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in {{ill|Sano|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintarō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yorimichi|qq=}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the {{ill|Taihō Code|ja|大宝律令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taihō Code|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for {{ill|Kintarō|ja|金太郎|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintarō|qq=}}. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=}} *: A warrior and retainer of {{ill|Fujiwara no Yorimichi|ja|藤原頼通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yorimichi|qq=}}.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|qq=}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|qq=}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|qq=}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. === Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|qq=}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|qq=}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|qq=}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

'''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."

His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|qq=}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|qq=}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
