A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|qq=|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|qq=|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].

[[ja:下毛野国造]]
<!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11361048}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|qq=|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|qq=|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}

Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. == Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|qq=|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].

[[ja:下毛野国造]]
<!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11361048}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}

Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

[[ja:下毛野国造]]
<!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--><!--Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:Shimotsukeno clan]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11361048}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:下毛野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q11361048]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Shimotsukenu no Kuninomiyatsuko''' (下毛野国造) was the ruler of {{ill|Shimotsukenokuni|ja|下毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukenokuni|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下毛野国造|1=Shimotsukeno no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361048}}, an area that later became [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]]. Originally part of the {{ill|Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it split into {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|上毛野国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko|1=Kamitsuke no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11359471}} and Shimotsukeno no Kuninomiyatsuko during the reigns of Emperor Nintoku and Emperor Kōtoku.
== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}

== Overview ==
=== Naming === The title appears as '''Shimotsukeno Kimi''' in the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], and as '''Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko''' in the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'s "Kuninomiyatsuko Hongi."
=== Ancestors === * According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja|豊木入日子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiiribikonomikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 豊城入彦命|1=Prince Toyokiirihiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633375}}, a son of Emperor Sujin, was the ancestor of both the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. * The [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] states that Emperor Sujin's son, {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, was tasked with governing the eastern provinces and became the ancestor of the Kamitsukeno and Shimotsukeno clans. His grandson, {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, served as Governor-General of the 15 provinces of the [[:en:Tōsandō|Tōsandō]] under Emperor Keikō, and his son, {{ill|Mimorowake no Ō|ja|御諸別王|1=Mimorowake|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Mimorowake|qq=}}, continued his work. * The [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]] states that during Emperor Nintoku’s reign, {{ill|Narawake|ja|奈良別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Narawake|1=Narawake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17227081}}, a fourth-generation descendant of Toyokineiribikonomikoto, was appointed Shimotsukeno Kuninomiyatsuko.
=== Clan === The {{ill|Shimotsukeno clan|ja|下毛野氏|1=Shimotsukeno clan|lt=Shimotsukeno clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno clan|qq=}} (Shimotsukeno-uji), whose title was {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|ja|君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi (kabane)|qq=}}. In the 13th year of Emperor Tenmu’s reign (684 CE), they were granted the title of [[:en:Ason|Asomi]]. In Enryaku 2 (783 CE), the Shimotsukeno name was bestowed upon subjugated Emishi.
== Headquarters == The headquarters of the Kuninomiyatsuko is believed to have been in the vicinity of present-day [[:en:Utsunomiya|Utsunomiya]].
=== Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）

A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. === Territory === The territory of the Kuninomiyatsuko, known at the time as '''Shimotsukeno no Kuni''', later became Shimotsuke Province excluding the {{ill|Nasu Province|ja|那須国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nasu Province|1=Nasu Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642906}}. It corresponds to the southwestern part of modern-day [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]].
In antiquity, the basin of the [[:en:Kinugawa River|Kinugawa River]], then called Kenogawa, flowed into [[:en:Katori Sea|Katori Sea]]. A powerful clan descended from Emperor Sujin's son Toyokineiribikonomikoto ruled this area, known as {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 毛野|1=Keno Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1886428}}, exerting strong influence within the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kinship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}. The province was later divided into Kamitsukeno (modern Gunma Prefecture) and Shimotsukeno.
== Clan Shrines == * [[:en:Futarasan shrine|Nikkō Futarasan Shrine]] *: Located in [[:en:Nikkō|Nikkō]], Tochigi Prefecture. Its deities include {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Tagorihime|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}}, and {{ill|Ame no Hohi|ja|味耜高彦根命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-hohi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}}, ancestors of the Kuninomiyatsuko. Recognized as the ichinomiya of Shimotsuke Province. * [[:en:Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine|Utsunomiya Futarayama Shrine]] *: Located in Utsunomiya, Tochigi Prefecture. Dedicated to {{ill|Toyokineiribikonomikoto|ja|豊城入彦命|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|1=Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokineiribikonomikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokiirihiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and others, it is also an ichinomiya. * {{ill|Akagi Shrine|ja|赤城神社|1=Akagi Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Akagi Shrines|qq=}} *: Located in [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano]], Tochigi Prefecture. Enshrines {{ill|Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosashima no Ōkimi|lt=Hikosasima no Ō|ja|彦狭嶋王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikosasima no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 彦狭島王}}, another ancestor.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Komaro|ja|下毛野古麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Komaro|qq=}} *: An official of the Asuka period, involved in drafting the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Code]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|ja|下毛野虫麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Mushimaro|qq=}} *: A Nara-period scholar and poet. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|ja|下毛野年継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|1=Shimotsukeno no Toshitsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q59551822}} *: A noble of the Heian period, attaining the Rank of Fifth Rank Senior. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|ja|下毛野公時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Tomonari|1=Shimotsukeno no Kintoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17992898}} *: A warrior of the Heian period, believed to be the model for [[:en:Kintarō|Kintarō]]. * {{ill|Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja|下毛野公忠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 下野公忠|1=Shimotsukeno no Kimichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361379}} *: A warrior and retainer of [[:en:Fujiwara no Yorimichi|Fujiwara no Yorimichi]].
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kuninomiyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == * 『國史大辭典』（吉川弘文館） * 坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』（吉川弘文館） * 『日本史広辞典』（山川出版社） * 『神道大辞典』（臨川書店）
